name: Cotillas — circular route along the Guadalimar river
name_original: Cotillas — circular por el río Guadalimar
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Cotillas offer a diverse landscape for riders, characterized by a mix of fertile river plains, arid "badlands," and green pine groves. The region features a relief of low altitude with small elevations, gently sloping towards the Segura River valley. This topography provides both flat, riverside trails and more undulating terrain, including unique geological formations like those found in Rambla Salada. Best mountain bike trails around Cotillas The most popular mountain bike trail is The Cerro de los Calarejos from Siles - circular route through the Natural Park of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura and las Villas, a 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses a significant natural park, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Segura de la Sierra from Siles - circular via the Morles river, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This route follows the Morles river, providing scenic views and challenging segments. Local mountain bikers also love the Dehesa del Oso and Alto de Cambrón Loop from Siles, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) trail leading through varied natural landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Mountain biking around Cotillas is defined by routes along the Segura River, through arid "badlands," and within areas featuring unique geological formations like Rambla Salada. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging difficult routes. The routes in Cotillas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cotillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cotillas?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Cotillas, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Cotillas?
A: The trails around Cotillas cater to various skill levels. You'll find 4 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 27 difficult routes, ensuring options for both beginners and experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on mountain bike trails around Cotillas?
A: Mountain bike trails around Cotillas offer a diverse landscape, from the fertile orchards of the Segura River plain to more arid 'badlands' and green pine and almond groves. The topography includes low-altitude elevations, gently sloping towards the Segura River valley, providing a mix of flat riverside trails and undulating terrain.

Q: Are there any unique geological features to see while mountain biking in Cotillas?
A: Yes, the region features unique geological formations, particularly in areas like Rambla Salada. Here, you can encounter spectacular natural spaces with canyons, vertical walls, saline springs, and distinctive dark-colored soils, creating a 'typical wetland' environment with a rugged, visually interesting terrain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cotillas?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the Vía Verde del Noroeste, which passes through the wider region, is known for its gentle gradients and natural road, making it comfortable and safe for cyclists of all ages, including families.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cotillas?
A: Yes, many routes around Cotillas are circular. For example, you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211840924/the-cerro-de-los-calarejos-from-siles-circular-route-through-the-natural-park-of-the-sierra-de-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cerro de los Calarejos from Siles&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211915642/dehesa-del-oso-and-alto-de-cambron-loop-from-siles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa del Oso and Alto de Cambrón Loop from Siles&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cotillas?
A: The routes in Cotillas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, from river plains to unique geological formations, and the challenging yet rewarding nature of many routes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions accessible by mountain bike?
A: Beyond the unique landscapes of Rambla Salada and the Segura River, you can find stunning views from various mountain peaks. Consider routes that pass near highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5909433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrón Peak&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Cotillas has a rich history. You can find historical sites like the restored Church of Our Lady of La Salceda or the Hermitage of the Cross. Some routes might also pass near remnants of Roman or Moorish settlements. For specific historical structures, you might find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra Ruins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Cotillas?
A: The Region of Murcia generally offers favorable conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with pleasant temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, though it's always wise to check local weather forecasts for specific conditions.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails around Cotillas?
A: While popular routes attract many, the extensive network of over 50 trails means you can often find quieter paths. Exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate or difficult routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1212166772/piedra-del-cambron-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedra del Cambrón Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more secluded experience.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that follow rivers or have water features?
A: Absolutely. The western part of the municipality is traversed by the Segura River, offering routes along its fertile plains. Additionally, the unique Rambla Salada area features a small spring of salt water and a channel carved by meteorological forces, creating interesting water formations and even small waterfalls in certain sections.
tips: A viewpoint of the town of Cotillas and the Castillo de la Hiedra on the mountain above it. To get to the ruins you will have to climb a single path, but the views are worth it.
Single and double track path that ascends to the Castillo de la Hiedra from Cotillas. The trail crosses a landscape of pine forests until reaching the ruins that dominate the valley.

waypoints.name: View of Cotillas and Castillo de la Hiedra
